title: Occlusion-Based Object Transportation Around Obstacles With a Swarm of Miniature Robots
abstract: Extends the classic Chen et al. occlusion-based transport strategy: simple 8 cm robots push a 5 kg object toward a goal they cannot always see. The new trick is that any robot losing sight of the goal can convert itself into a stationary sub-goal (turning goal-colored), spontaneously forming a chain of beacons around obstacles. Fully decentralized, communication-free, vision-only. With 15+ robots: 100 percent completion around a corner; 94 percent with two corners; concave H-shaped objects remain hard (46 percent). Simulation study (Atta), open-source code.
user_notes: Verified by full-text read. HONEST FRAMING REQUIRED: this is simulation-only, say so plainly. The story is the mechanism: robots that lose sight of the goal BECOME the goal, chaining sub-goal beacons around obstacles with zero communication. Concrete numbers to use: 8 cm robots, 5 kg object, 100 percent completion around a corner with 15+ robots, concave H-shape drops to 46 percent. Thread question that will drive replies: the authors' own teleoperated benchmark still beats it on efficiency, and sim-to-hardware for vision-only occlusion sensing is the open gap. Link the repo (has the simulator + figures).
